questions.q: What does the Sanskrit word 'Purana' traditionally mean?
questions.explanation: The word 'Purana' means 'ancient' or 'that which is old', reflecting the texts' focus on tales of ancient times.

questions.q: How many Mahapuranas (great Puranas) are traditionally counted in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Hindu tradition traditionally counts eighteen Mahapuranas, the major works of the Puranic genre.

questions.q: To which broad category of Hindu scripture do the Puranas belong?
questions.explanation: The Puranas are classed as smriti, the remembered tradition, distinct from the shruti of the Vedas.

questions.q: Which celebrated devotional Purana centres on the life and deeds of Krishna?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana is especially beloved for its account of Krishna's life and is a central text of Vaishnava devotion.

questions.q: The Puranas are regarded as the primary source of what aspect of Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: The Puranas are the primary source of popular Hindu mythology, narrating the deeds of deities and avatars.

questions.q: Which Purana is named for and largely devoted to the deity Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu Purana is one of the major Mahapuranas, centred on Vishnu and his avatars.

questions.q: The Puranas narrate the deeds of the avatars of which preserver deity in particular?
questions.explanation: The Puranas extensively narrate the avatars of Vishnu, such as Rama, Krishna, Narasimha, and Vamana.

questions.q: Which Purana shares its name with the destroyer deity of the Trimurti?
questions.explanation: The Shiva Purana is a major Mahapurana devoted to the deity Shiva and his lore.

questions.q: In what language were the classical Mahapuranas composed?
questions.explanation: The classical Mahapuranas were composed in Sanskrit, the sacred language of much of Hindu scripture.

questions.q: The Puranas frequently recount which famous churning of the cosmic ocean?
options: The dice game of the Pandavas
questions.explanation: The Samudra Manthan, the churning of the ocean of milk for amrita, is a celebrated episode recounted in the Puranas.

questions.q: The classical Puranas are traditionally defined by how many characteristic marks, the pancha-lakshana?
questions.explanation: The pancha-lakshana, or five marks, traditionally define what a Purana should contain.

questions.q: Which of these is one of the five traditional marks (pancha-lakshana) of a Purana?
questions.explanation: The genealogies of gods and kings (vamsha) are among the five traditional marks of a Purana.

questions.q: Which Purana is the source of the renowned Devi Mahatmya, glorifying the Goddess?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya, celebrating the Goddess Durga's victory over Mahishasura, is embedded in the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: The Puranas describe the creation and dissolution of the cosmos through which vast time cycles?
questions.explanation: Puranic cosmology unfolds through immense cycles of yugas and kalpas, marking creation and dissolution.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, how many ages or yugas make up one complete cycle, the Mahayuga?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe four yugas, Satya, Treta, Dvapara, and Kali, that together form one Mahayuga.

questions.q: Which sage is traditionally credited with compiling and arranging the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The sage Vyasa, also credited with arranging the Vedas, is traditionally said to have compiled the Puranas.

questions.q: Which Purana is traditionally associated with rites concerning death and the afterlife?
questions.explanation: The Garuda Purana is well known for its sections on death, funeral rites, and the journey of the soul.

questions.q: The Bhagavata Purana describes how many principal avatars of Vishnu in its famous listing?
questions.explanation: The Puranic tradition, including the Bhagavata, popularly enumerates the Dashavatara, the ten principal avatars of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which Mahapurana is the longest, traditionally regarded as the most voluminous of the eighteen?
questions.explanation: The Skanda Purana is traditionally counted as the largest of the eighteen Mahapuranas.

questions.q: Besides the eighteen Mahapuranas, what is the name given to the secondary or minor Puranas?
questions.explanation: Alongside the eighteen Mahapuranas, tradition recognises a further set of minor texts called the Upapuranas.

questions.q: The Puranas commonly describe the deity Vishnu reclining upon which cosmic serpent between creations?
questions.explanation: Puranic accounts depict Vishnu reclining on the great serpent Ananta-Shesha upon the cosmic ocean.

questions.q: Which avatar story, in which Vishnu measures the cosmos in three strides, is recounted in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas narrate Vamana, the dwarf avatar of Vishnu, who covers the worlds in three strides to subdue Bali.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the Mahapuranas are traditionally classified into three groups associated with which scheme?
questions.explanation: Tradition groups the eighteen Mahapuranas into three sets of six, associated with the gunas sattva, rajas, and tamas.

questions.q: Which Purana opens with the Matsya (fish) avatar rescuing Manu from the great flood, and is named for that avatar?
questions.explanation: The Matsya Purana takes its name from the fish avatar of Vishnu, who saves Manu and the seeds of creation from the deluge.

questions.q: Which Purana is named after the tortoise avatar that supported Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean?
questions.explanation: The Kurma Purana is named for the tortoise avatar of Vishnu, who bore Mount Mandara during the Samudra Manthan.

questions.q: Within the Puranic framework, which Manu is said to preside over the present age of humanity?
questions.explanation: The Puranas hold that the present manvantara is presided over by Vaivasvata Manu, the seventh Manu.

questions.q: Which Purana, named for a son of Shiva, is celebrated for its many regional and sthala (place) legends?
questions.explanation: The Skanda Purana, named for Skanda (Kartikeya), is renowned for its abundant sthala-mahatmyas, legends of sacred sites.

questions.q: The Puranas teach that at the close of a kalpa the cosmos undergoes which periodic dissolution?
questions.explanation: Puranic cosmology describes pralaya, the periodic dissolution of the cosmos at the end of a kalpa, before re-creation.

questions.q: Which Purana shares its name with Vishnu's eagle mount and is framed as his dialogue with that bird?
questions.explanation: The Garuda Purana is framed as a discourse from Vishnu to his eagle mount Garuda, after whom it is named.

questions.q: Which Purana takes the form of a dialogue revealed by the god Agni and serves as an encyclopaedic compendium?
questions.explanation: The Agni Purana, traditionally revealed by Agni, is encyclopaedic in scope, covering ritual, cosmology, and many arts and sciences.
